| Elo min date    = 2 June 2001

| pattern_so2     = _pumablack
| leftarm2        = FFFFFF
| body2           = FFFFFF
| rightarm2       = FFFFFF
| shorts2         = FFFFFF
| socks2          = FFFFFF
| pattern_la3     = _puma_white
| pattern_b3      = _aze25t
| pattern_ra3     = _puma_white
| pattern_sh3     = _pumawhite
| pattern_so3     = _pumawhite
| leftarm3        = c90000
| body3           = FF0000
| rightarm3       = c90000
| shorts3         = FF0000
| socks3          = FF0000
| First game      = '''Official'''<br>{{fb|GEO|1990}} 6–3 {{fb-rt|AZE|1990}}<br>([[Gurjaani]], [[Georgia (country)|Georgia]]; 17 September 1992)
| Largest win     = {{fb|AZE}} 6–1 {{fb-rt|Saint Lucia}}<br>([[Sumgait]], [[Azerbaijan]]; 27 March 2026)
| Largest loss    = {{fb|FRA|1974}} [[France v Azerbaijan (UEFA Euro 1996 qualifying)|10–0]] {{fb-rt|AZE}}<br>([[Auxerre]], [[France]]; 6 September 1995)
}}

The '''Azerbaijan national football team''' ({{langx|az|Azərbaycan milli futbol komandası}}) is the national [[association football|football]] team of [[Azerbaijan]] and is controlled by [[Association of Football Federations of Azerbaijan]]. It represents Azerbaijan in international football competitions. The majority of Azerbaijan's home matches are held at the national stadium, [[Baku Olympic Stadium]], with [[friendly match]]es sometimes hosted at club stadiums.

The Azerbaijan national football team has taken part in qualification for each major tournament since [[UEFA Euro 1996|Euro 1996]], but has never qualified for the finals tournament of any [[FIFA World Cup|World Cup]] or [[UEFA European Championship|European Championships]]. Azerbaijan was the first [[Caucasus]] country to host a major football tournament, the [[UEFA Euro 2020]].

==History==
===Early period (before the 1920s)===
In the early twentieth century, football began to become popular in Azerbaijan, which was then part of the [[Russian Empire]]. In 1912, Azerbaijani football players had their first "international match" and they won 4–2 in [[Tbilisi]], [[Georgia (country)|Georgia]] against the local "Sokol" team. During 1912–1913, matches between the Azerbaijani and Georgian football teams were organized, first in Tbilisi and then in Baku. In 1914, the Football Union was founded in Azerbaijan. The Football Union undertook the organization of official city championships and other competitions.<ref name="Azerbaijans">{{cite web|title=Football|url=http://www.azerbaijans.com/content_675_en.html|work=Azerbaijans.az|access-date=17 September 2012|archive-date=22 April 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180422131304/http://www.azerbaijans.com/content_675_en.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

===Soviet era (1920s–1991)===
The oldest records of football teams in [[Azerbaijan Soviet Socialist Republic|Soviet Azerbaijan]] go back to 1926–1927, when [[Transcaucasian Socialist Federative Soviet Republic|Trans-Caucasian]] Championship was organized in Tbilisi. Three [[South Caucasia]]n countries participated: Azerbaijan, Armenia and Georgia.<ref name="ELO">{{Cite web |url=http://www.eloratings.net/Azerbaijan.htm |title=World Football Elo Ratings: Azerbaijan |access-date=18 July 2008 |archive-date=19 July 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170719224335/http://eloratings.net/Azerbaijan.htm |url-status=live }}</ref> The Azerbaijan national football team held its first friendly matches against [[Georgia national football team|Georgia]] and [[Armenia national football team|Armenia]] in 1927 for the Trans-Caucasian Championship in Georgia.<ref name="ELO"/> Also in 1926, football players from Azerbaijan played three matches with [[Tehran XI|Football team from Iran]] in Baku. In 1929, three matches were played between these teams in Tehran. In all matches Azerbaijan players won.<ref>Д. М. Векилов. Участие Азербайджанской ССР в развитии советско-иранских торгово-экономических и культурных связей в 20—30-х годах // Известия Академии наук Азербайджанской ССР.&nbsp;— 1986.&nbsp;— С. 41.</ref><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.affa.az/index.php?r=8&lang=az |title=Azərbaycan futbolunun bir əsrlik tarixindən səhifələr |access-date=6 July 2013 |archive-date=8 September 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170908021702/http://www.affa.az/index.php?r=8&lang=az |url-status=live }}</ref>

The 1960s is considered the [[Golden Age]] for Azerbaijani football as it produced great players like [[Anatoliy Banishevskiy]], [[Alakbar Mammadov]] and the football referee [[Tofiq Bahramov]], most famous for being the [[Assistant referee (association football)|linesman]] who helped to award a goal for England in the [[1966 World Cup Final]] between [[English national football team|England]] and [[German national football team|West Germany]].

===Post-independence (1992–present)===
====1990s====
After [[Azerbaijan]] gained its independence in 1991, AFFA&nbsp;— [[Association of Football Federations of Azerbaijan]]&nbsp;— was created. In 1992, renowned Azerbaijani footballer [[Alekper Mamedov]] became the first head coach of the Azerbaijani national football team, compiling a 3–1 record as coach that includes the first ever national team victory, over [[Georgia national football team|Georgia]] on 25 May 1993.<ref name="coach1">
{{cite web
 |last=Ibragimov
 |first=Erkin
 |title=History: 1992
 |publisher=AzeriFootball.com
 |url=http://www.azerifootball.com/contents/default.aspx?lg=2&idpt=1992
 |access-date=3 July 2008
 |url-status=dead
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071212144114/http://azerifootball.com/contents/default.aspx?lg=2&idpt=1992
 |archive-date=12 December 2007
}}</ref><ref name="coach2">
{{cite web
 |last=Ibragimov
 |first=Erkin
 |title=History: 1993
 |publisher=AzeriFootball.com
 |url=http://www.azerifootball.com/contents/default.aspx?lg=2&idpt=1993
 |access-date=3 July 2008
 |url-status=dead
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071212144147/http://azerifootball.com/contents/default.aspx?lg=2&idpt=1993
 |archive-date=12 December 2007
}}</ref> The national team was accepted as a provisional member of [[UEFA]] on 2 December 1993, and later as a full member in May 1994 at the 22nd [[UEFA Congress]] in [[Vienna]].<ref>{{Cite news |date=2 December 1993 |title=UEFA unveils European Cup revamp |url=https://www.newspapers.com/article/grimsby-evening-telegraph-uefa-unveils-e/180518122/ |access-date=6 September 2025 |work=[[Grimsby Evening Telegraph]] |location=Grimsby, Humberside, England |page=48 |via=[[Newspapers.com]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |date=2 May 1994 |title=Elected as full members of UEFA |url=https://www.newspapers.com/article/the-los-angeles-times-elected-as-full-me/180518415/ |access-date=6 September 2025 |work=[[The Los Angeles Times]] |location=Los Angeles, California, United States |page=137 |via=[[Newspapers.com]]}}</ref> It also joined [[FIFA]] in June 1994 at the 49th [[FIFA Congress]] in [[Chicago]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Azərbaycan futbolu 100: 100 years of football in Azerbaijan |url=http://www.affa.az/uploads_docs/Affa%20Book4.pdf |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200724014743/https://www.affa.az/uploads_docs/Affa%20Book4.pdf |archive-date=24 July 2020 |access-date=6 September 2025 |publisher=[[Association of Football Federations of Azerbaijan]]}}</ref> The security issues forced the team to play all of its home [[UEFA Euro 1996]] qualifiers in [[Trabzon, Turkey]], after UEFA imposed a ban on home matches on 21 October 1994.<ref name="euro96">
{{cite web
 |last=Stokkermans
 |first=Karel
 |title=European Championship 1996
 |publisher=Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation
 |date=18 January 2000
 |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/96e.html
 |access-date=7 July 2008
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080707104033/http://www.rsssf.com/tables/96e.html
 |archive-date=7 July 2008
 |url-status=live
}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |date=22 October 1994 |title=Azerbaijan ban |url=https://www.newspapers.com/article/manchester-evening-news-azerbaijan-ban/180599603/ |access-date=7 September 2025 |work=[[Manchester Evening News]] |location=Manchester, Greater Manchester, England |page=47 |via=[[Newspapers.com]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |date=9 November 1994 |title=At home on road |url=https://www.newspapers.com/article/the-toronto-star-at-home-on-road/180599715/ |access-date=7 September 2025 |work=[[The Toronto Star]] |location=Toronto, Ontario, Canada |page=74 |via=[[Newspapers.com]]}}</ref>

====2000s====
[[File:Iceland-Azerbaijan.JPG|thumbnail|left|Azerbaijani squad in 2008.]]
As of the early 2000s, Azerbaijani football federation started to integrate more players to the national team through [[FIFA eligibility rules|FIFA's eligibility rules]].<ref>{{cite web|script-title=ru:Это не те немцы. И футбол они дают неправильный|url=http://www.azerifootball.com/ru/17/news/22142.html|work=azerifootball.com|access-date=7 November 2013|language=ru|archive-date=10 April 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180410134726/http://www.azerifootball.com/ru/17/news/22142.html|url-status=live}}</ref> In February 2004, [[Carlos Alberto Torres]], captain of the [[Brazil national football team|Brazil]] team that won the [[1970 FIFA World Cup]] was appointed its national coach. Despite a poor start, a 0–6 defeat to [[Israel national football team|Israel]] on February 18, Azerbaijan won their first ever away match, 3–2 against [[Kazakhstan national football team|Kazakhstan]] on April 28. In June 2005, following a 3–0 defeat by [[Poland national football team|Poland]], Torres stood down from the position, to be replaced by former [[Neftchi PFK|Neftchi]] coach [[Vagif Sadygov]], his third spell as coach of Azerbaijan. [[Shahin Diniyev]] took over as manager in November 2005. He resigned on 31 October 2007, and [[Gjoko Hadzievski]] was named as care-taking coach of Azerbaijan.<ref>[http://en.euro2008.uefa.com/index.html Euro 2008 homepage] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100204053458/http://en.euro2008.uefa.com/index.html |date=February 4, 2010 }}, ''[[UEFA]]''</ref>

In April 2008, former German football player and coach [[Berti Vogts]] was appointed as a manager of Azerbaijan on a two-year contract.<ref name="vogts">[http://www.uefa.com/competitions/worldcup/news/kind=1/newsid=680090.html FIFA World Cup News and Features] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080408020913/http://www.uefa.com/competitions/worldcup/news/kind%3D1/newsid%3D680090.html |date=April 8, 2008 }}, ''[[UEFA]]''</ref> Azerbaijan had a mixed qualifying campaign, finishing with 5 points, just missing out on a last place to [[Liechtenstein national football team|Liechtenstein]] with 2 points.

In November 2009, [[Association of Football Federations of Azerbaijan|AFFA]] extended Berti Vogts' contract for a further two years, making him the first manager to manage the weak Azerbaijani national team in two qualification cycles.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20091113053139/http://www.fifa.com/worldfootball/news/newsid=1131508.html#vogts+contract+extended fifa.com: Vogts' contract extended]</ref><ref>[http://ru.uefa.com/competitions/worldcup/news/kind=1/newsid=919032.html Фогтс увидел перспективу]{{dead link|date=March 2025|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}} {{in lang|ru}}</ref>

====2010s====
In 2010, following a shock win over [[Turkey national football team|Turkey]], the team reached 90th place in [[FIFA World Rankings]], Azerbaijan's highest ever position in the country's football history.<ref>{{cite web|title=FIFA/Coca-Cola World Ranking: Azerbaijan|url=https://www.fifa.com/associations/association=aze/ranking/gender=m/index.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070630032905/http://www.fifa.com/associations/association=aze/ranking/gender=m/index.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=30 June 2007|publisher=[[FIFA]]|access-date=1 December 2010}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Azərgaycan yığması FİFA reytinqində rekord pilləyə yüksəlib|url=http://www.antenn.az/article11333.html|publisher=antenn.az|access-date=1 December 2010|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101212074058/http://antenn.az/article11333.html|archive-date=12 December 2010|url-status=dead}}</ref> After victory over [[Kazakhstan national football team|Kazakhstan]], Azerbaijan also broke their scoring and points records by gaining 7 points and scoring 10 goals.<ref>{{cite web|title=Qələbə, qisas və 1 gündə daha 2 rekord&nbsp;– YENİLƏNİB|url=http://apasport.az/xeber_Q%C9%99l%C9%99b%C9%99,_qisas_v%C9%99_1_g%C3%BCnd%C9%99_daha_2_rekord_-_YEN%C4%B0L%C6%8FN%C4%B0B_50160.html|work=apasport.az|access-date=6 September 2011|language=az|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120325091802/http://apasport.az/xeber_Q%C9%99l%C9%99b%C9%99,_qisas_v%C9%99_1_g%C3%BCnd%C9%99_daha_2_rekord_-_YEN%C4%B0L%C6%8FN%C4%B0B_50160.html|archive-date=25 March 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Azerbaijan beat Kazakhstan to set points record|url=http://www.news.az/articles/sports/43836|work=news.az|access-date=7 September 2011|archive-date=21 December 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111221114414/http://news.az/articles/sports/43836|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In November 2011, AFFA extended Berti Vogts' contract for a further two years, until the end of the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|2014 FIFA World Cup qualification cycle]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Aliyev |first=Elmir |script-title=ru:Определилась судьба Берти Фогтса в сборной Азербайджана |url=http://www.1news.az/sport/football/20111130020845046.html |work=1news.az |access-date=30 November 2011 |language=ru |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111203035535/http://1news.az/sport/football/20111130020845046.html |archive-date=3 December 2011 }}</ref> Under Vogts, Azerbaijan had some poor results, not being able to defeat second-string sides. Vogts faced major criticism, protest and demonstration from local supporters and the media.<ref>{{cite web|script-title=ru:Кто тут "карлик"?|url=http://www.azerisport.com/football/20130322091243249.html|work=Azerisport.com|access-date=22 March 2013|language=ru|archive-date=10 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180210002411/http://www.azerisport.com/football/20130322091243249.html|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Futbolumuz sürətlə inkişaf edir!!! |url=http://fanat.az/cirtdan-ovu-yenil%C6%8Fnir/ |work=Fanat.az |access-date=22 March 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130331122738/http://fanat.az/cirtdan-ovu-yenil%C6%8Fnir/ |archive-date=31 March 2013 }}</ref> However, Azerbaijan managed to finish the qualification cycle in fourth place, the team's best ever finish.<ref>{{cite web|title=Russia secure point to book finals place|url=http://www.uefa.com/worldcup/season=2014/matches/round=2000294/match=2008668/postmatch/report/index.html|work=[[UEFA]]|access-date=15 October 2013|archive-date=9 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180209182347/http://www.uefa.com/worldcup/season=2014/matches/round=2000294/match=2008668/postmatch/report/index.html|url-status=dead}}</ref> In December 2013, Vogts being granted a new two-year contract, with the aim to lead Azerbaijan through [[UEFA Euro 2016|EURO 2016]] qualifying.<ref>{{cite web|title=Vogts gets new Azergayjan mandate|url=https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/news/0253-0d7f0fa79b3f-38982a579d08-1000--vogts-gets-new-azerbaijan-mandate/|work=[[UEFA]]|date=19 December 2013|access-date=26 December 2013|archive-date=7 February 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150207080419/http://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/qualifiers/news/newsid=2039485.html|url-status=live}}</ref> In July 2014, Azerbaijan beat its ranking record by reaching 73rd place in FIFA World Rankings. Following three straight losses, Vogts resigned from his post after spending six years in charge of Azerbaijan.<ref>{{cite web|title=Vogts leaves Azerbaijan post|url=http://www.football-italia.net/57335/vogts-leaves-azerbaijan-post|website=www.football-italia.net|date=17 October 2014 |access-date=17 October 2014|archive-date=18 October 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141018041832/http://football-italia.net/57335/vogts-leaves-azerbaijan-post|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Vogts quits Azerbaijan helm|url=https://www.uefa.com/european-qualifiers/news/0253-0d7fb85ab069-71e5cfe207ac-1000--vogts-quits-azerbaijan-helm/|website=[[UEFA]]|date=17 October 2014|access-date=17 October 2014|archive-date=20 October 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141020030847/http://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/qualifiers/news/newsid=2166708.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

Succeeding Vogts as full-time manager was former [[Croatia national football team|Croatia]] international [[Robert Prosinečki]]. He guided the Azerbaijani team to another record points haul (10) in [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualifying]], but the team still finished fifth in the six-team Group C. Prosinečki resigned after deciding not to extend his contract with the Azerbaijan Football Federation and was succeeded by countryman [[Nikola Jurčević]].

====2020s====
The [[UEFA Euro 2020 qualifying]] proved to be a disaster for Azerbaijan as the team finished in bottom with a complete seven defeats, including a famous 1–5 at home against Slovakia, and only one draw, which was, surprisingly, against [[2018 FIFA World Cup]] runners-up Croatia, the home of the manager. Nikola Jurčević departed following the poor performance of Azerbaijan. After the qualifying, Azerbaijan turned out to be the only country ever to not qualify for a tournament they co-hosted.

The qualification for the [[2022 FIFA World Cup]] with new coach [[Gianni De Biasi]] also went horribly, with Azerbaijan getting only a single point in Group A in matches with strongholds Portugal and Serbia and secondary sides Ireland and Luxembourg. The [[2022-23 UEFA Nations League|Nations League]] that year was more successful. Azerbaijan was drawn into Group 3 of League C with Kazakhstan, Belarus and Slovakia. After failing to win against Kazakhstan and Slovakia and securing themselves a goalless draw against Belarus, the matches of the second leg were all wins. At the end, Azerbaijan finished second with 10 points, three points away from promoted first-place Kazakhstan. After losing three matches in the [[UEFA Euro 2024 qualifying]] and drawing once, De Biasi got sacked on 22 October 2023. The games without a manager were equal: Azerbaijan won 2 matches out of four, including a surprising 3-0 defeat of already-eliminated Sweden. The other two matches were both lost.

In March 2024, the assistant manager [[Arif Asadov]] was hired as caretaker manager. Under Asadov, the team won two out of 4 games. In June 2024, Former [[UEFA Euro 2016]] winning coach [[Fernando Santos (footballer, born 1954)|Fernando Santos]] was appointed as the new manager. The [[2024-25 UEFA Nations League]] went badly, securing only a draw against Estonia and getting relegated. Azerbaijan also failed to qualify for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup]], being in a group with 2022 runners-up France and Iceland and Ukraine, with the latter two failing to qualify for the 2022 edition. They only secured a single point in a 1-1 draw against Ukraine.

==Team image==
===Colours===
[[File:Azerbaijan nationall football team.JPG|thumb|right|Azerbaijan national football team in October 2013.]]
As of [[UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying|UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying round]], Azerbaijan's home colours are all-red kit. The team wears an all-blue kit for away games. This combination of colours is traditional for the national team since their first game. At the beginning of 90s, the team wore a white shirt, but towards the end of the decade it was transformed into blue-white striped shirts. At the beginning of the 2000s, the kit was replaced by the white shirt with a vertical tricolour stripe, formed of the colors from the [[Flag of Azerbaijan|national flag of Azerbaijan]] on the chest. The kit was changed into blue shirts, red shorts and green socks only for the [[UEFA Euro 2008 qualifying|UEFA Euro 2008 qualifying round]]. After the tournament, the national team went back to their usual combination of colours.

Azerbaijan national team's away colours were yellow-black striped shirts, black shorts and yellow socks until [[UEFA Euro 2004 qualifying|UEFA Euro 2004 qualifying round]], when it was decided to abandon this kit in favor of the completely blue. During the [[UEFA Euro 2008 qualifying|UEFA Euro 2008 qualifying round]] games the team has used an all red kit.

Since 2017, Azerbaijan's kit have been supplied by [[Nike, Inc.|Nike]]. They took over from [[Puma AG|Puma]], who were Azerbaijan's kit suppliers between 2004 and 2006. Before that [[Umbro]] were Azerbaijan's kit suppliers between 2002 and 2004.

===Nickname===
Azerbaijan is often referred to by the media and supporters as ''Milli'' (The National), which is the nickname associated with all of Azerbaijan's international sporting teams due to the team's utilization of the country's national colors.<ref>{{cite web|last=Sobolev|first=Ilya|script-title=ru:В Россию на заработки|url=http://www.rg.ru/2012/10/16/match-site.html|work=Rg.ru|date=16 October 2012|access-date=27 August 2013|language=ru|archive-date=17 May 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170517101144/https://rg.ru/2012/10/16/match-site.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

===Stadium===
{{main|Tofiq Bahramov Stadium|Baku Olympic Stadium}}

Most of Azerbaijan's home matches are played at the new [[Baku Olympic Stadium]] in Baku. It has been Azerbaijan's primary home stadium ever since the move from [[Tofiq Bahramov Stadium]] in 2015. Today, some qualifying matches and [[exhibition game|friendly match]]es are still hosted at the [[Tofiq Bahramov Stadium]], whereas others are hosted at the [[Lankaran City Stadium]], [[Bakcell Arena]] and [[Dalga Arena]] after it met [[UEFA]] stadium criteria.<ref>[http://azerisport.com/articles.php?item_id=20090717053108219&sec_id=2 Матч Азербайджан-Финляндия пройдет в Лянкяране] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111007050644/http://azerisport.com/articles.php?item_id=20090717053108219&sec_id=2 |date=7 October 2011 }} {{in lang|ru}}</ref><ref>[http://fanat.az/?mod=view&id=13989 Milli komandamız yenidən Lənkəran stadionunda] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180920011219/http://fanat.az/?mod=view&id=13989 |date=20 September 2018 }} {{in lang|az}}</ref>

===Media coverage and public relations===
Azerbaijan's matches are presently covered by the public channel [[İctimai Television|İTV]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Millinin oyunları AZTV və "İdman Azərbaycan"ın əlindən alındı və kimə verildi?|url=http://rekord.az/news/p:15015|website=rekord.az|access-date=14 August 2014|language=az|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170929044016/http://rekord.az/news/p:15015|archive-date=29 September 2017|url-status=dead}}</ref> [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification]] match rights were held by [[AZTV]] and [[Idman Azerbaijan TV]].<ref>{{cite web|title=UEFA yeni qərar qəbul etdi: qurum millimizin oyunlarını AzTV-dən alıb, İTV-yə verdi|url=http://korner.az/v3/xeber/9147/|website=korner.az|access-date=14 August 2014|language=az|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160107144105/http://korner.az/v3/xeber/9147/|archive-date=7 January 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref>

[[AZTV]], [[Idman Azerbaijan TV]] and [[Lider TV]] are among other networks that have previously shown live fixtures.<ref>{{cite web|title=UEFA millimizin oyunlarını AzTV-dən alıb, İTV-yə verdi|url=http://apasport.az/xeber_Millimizin_oyunlar%C4%B1_AzTV-d%C9%99n_al%C4%B1b,_%C4%B0TV-y%C9%99_verildi__73800.html|website=apasport.az|access-date=14 August 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140814124428/http://apasport.az/xeber_Millimizin_oyunlar%C4%B1_AzTV-d%C9%99n_al%C4%B1b,_%C4%B0TV-y%C9%99_verildi__73800.html|archive-date=14 August 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref>

|-

| 2017–2025
|-

|}

==Results and fixtures==
{{Main|Azerbaijan national football team results}}

{{Main|Azerbaijan national football team results (2020–present)}}

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===Coaching history===
[[File:Berti Vogts 1.JPG|thumbnail|220px|Under [[Berti Vogts]], Azerbaijan reached its highest position ever in FIFA World Rankings]]

==Players==
===Current squad===
*The following players have been called up for the [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)|2026 FIFA Series]] matches against [[Saint Lucia national football team|Saint Lucia]] and either [[Oman national football team|Oman]] or [[Sierra Leone national football team|Sierra Leone]] on 27 and 30 March 2026.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.affa.az/index.php/news/a-millinin-fifa-series-2026-n-heyti/80349|title=A millinin “FIFA Series – 2026” üçün heyəti|publisher=[[Association of Football Federations of Azerbaijan]]|date=20 March 2026|language=az}}</ref>

*''Caps and goals correct as of 27 March 2026, after the match against [[Saint Lucia national football team|Saint Lucia]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Shakhruddin Magomedaliyev]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|6|12|df=y}}|caps=35|goals=0|club=[[Qarabağ FK|Qarabağ]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Səlahət Ağayev]]|age={{birth date and age|1991|1|4|df=y}}|caps=22|goals=0|club=[[Qabala İK|Qabala]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Rza Jafarov]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|7|3|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[Neftçi PFK|Neftçi]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Aydın Bayramov]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|2|18|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Zira FK|Zira]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Badavi Guseynov]]|age={{birth date and age|1991|7|11|df=y}}|caps=78|goals=1|club=[[Qarabağ FK|Qarabağ]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Anton Kryvotsyuk]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|8|20|df=y}}|caps=45|goals=1|club=[[Daejeon Hana Citizen]]|clubnat=KOR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Elvin Cafarguliyev]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|10|26|df=y}}|caps=34|goals=1|club=[[Qarabağ FK|Qarabağ]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Rahil Mammadov]]|age={{birth date and age|1995|11|24|df=y}}|caps=25|goals=0|club=[[Araz-Naxçıvan PFK|Araz-Naxçıvan]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Qismət Alıyev]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|10|24|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=0|club=[[Zira FK|Zira]]|clubnat=AZE}}

;Notes
*<sup>INJ</sup> = Withdrew due to injury
<!-- *<sup>PRE</sup> = Preliminary squad / standby
*<sup>RET</sup> = Retired from the national team
*<sup>SUS</sup> = Serving suspension
*<sup>WD</sup> = Player withdrew from the squad due to non-injury issue. -->
{{nat fs end}}

==Honours==
===Friendly===
* '''Trans-Caucasian Championship'''
** '''Champions (2)''': 1926, 1927
* '''[[ECO Cup]]'''
** Third place (1): [[ECO Cup|1993]]
* '''UAE International Cup'''
** Third place (1): [[2009 UAE International Cup|2009]]
* '''FIFA Series'''
** Champions (1): [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)|2026]]
** Second place (1): [[2024 FIFA Series|2024]]
